text.title: AI Feature Brief Extension Pack Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules every AI extension pack enforces.

rule.statement: Model section pins name + version + provider + deprecation_window AND fallback_chain. "Latest claude" is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Floating model names = silent regression on provider updates.

rule.statement: Eval section pins golden_set_version + pass-rate threshold + ci_width + eval_method_ref. No "we'll test it manually".
rule.rationale: Without versioned eval, promotion decisions are guesswork.

rule.statement: Hallucination policy section enumerates risk classes (factual-with-source, numerical-summary, legal-implication, …) and picks one of {refuse, caveat, fall-through-to-human} per class. No "model decides".

rationale.source: EU AI Act Article 13; product safety practice

rule.rationale: Implicit policy means undefined behaviour at the worst moment.

rule.statement: Cost section names per-request soft cap, per-day hard cap, outlier-alert threshold, billing_owner. Unspecified caps = unbounded spend.

rationale.source: FinOps for ML; provider billing surprises 2026

rule.rationale: Without caps a runaway loop costs five-figure invoice in hours.

example.title: Pinned model section
example.code: chosen_model: claude-sonnet-4 provider: anthropic fallback_chain: [claude-sonnet-3.7, gpt-4o] deprecation_window_months: 12

example.title: Vague
example.code: Model: latest claude Eval: we'll keep an eye on it Policy: model handles it Cost: don't go crazy
